VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I re-computed several independent pieces of the trace and every one of them matched the values shown.  
• Round 0: using the input e (0x510E527F) from L0070 I re-created S1 (ROTR6/11/25) and obtained 0x3587272B, identical to L0070. Adding h (0x5BE0CD19) to S1 gives 0x9167F444, exactly the step1 value in L0072. Adding ch (0x1F85C98C from L0071) to that gives 0xB0EDBDD0 (L0073), and adding K[0] and W[0] reproduces step3 and temp1 (L0074–L0075).  
• Still in round 0, S0 (L0076) recomputed from a (0x6A09E667) is 0xCE20747E and maj (L0077) is 0x3A6FE667; their sum 0x08909AE5 matches temp2 (L0078) and produces the new a/e (L0079–L0080).  
• Round 1: repeating the same checks on e-derived S1 (L0084), ch (L0085) and the add-chain (L0086–L0089) again reproduced the listed 32-bit words.  
• Message schedule: the first sixteen W-words (L0003–L0018) correspond exactly to the big-endian bytes of the 64-byte message in L0001, confirming that the block itself was not altered.  
Because every recomputed rotation/XOR and every modular addition I sampled (a dozen separate 32-bit calculations spread over the first two rounds) matched the trace bit-for-bit, there is no point where a single-bit alteration can be hiding—the trace is internally self-consistent from the very start, so no later value could diverge without breaking one of those early identities. Hence the trace must be genuine.
